Journal.Title: Advances in colloid and interface science

Article.ArticleTitle: Multiscale interfacial dynamics in evaporation and boiling: A critical review of governing factors and heat transfer mechanisms.

Abstract.AbstractText: Liquid evaporation and boiling are ubiquitous in nature and industrial applications, yet a systematic understanding of multifactor influences on phase-change heat transfer remains elusive. The ability of a liquid to undergo phase change primarily depends on heat and mass transfer at the interface. This review provides the first comprehensive synthesis of governing factors across scales, critically analyzing internal elements (liquid properties, film thickness, homogeneous/heterogeneous additives) and external parameters (temperature, pressure, external fields, substrate characteristics) from macroscopic experiments to molecular dynamics simulations. By integrating multiscale approaches, including novel molecular dynamics models that reveal non-equilibrium interfacial heat-mass transfer mechanisms and disjoining pressure effects, how each factor enhances or inhibits phase transition is decoded. Significantly, the three-phase contact line as the pivotal region for interfacial transport is established and meniscus modeling methodologies are systematically consolidated. The work further examines synergistic factor interactions and unresolved challenges, offering fundamental insights for thermal engineering design in applications ranging from microelectronics cooling to aerospace systems.
